$comment: Synthetic UAT fixture for I-0040 (signal extraction). Two workstreams with deliberate noise: real decisions worth extracting, off-topic items, near-duplicates for re-shelve, a cross-workstream identity candidate for door-watch, and an idle cluster for manual dust. Timestamps are absolute so behavior is stable across CI runs.

workstreams.name: work
workstreams.description: Pat's day job at Acme — backend platform team. Focus: infrastructure decisions, on-call rotations, RFCs, eng-org logistics.

rows.subject: RFC-0042: Postgres for the new ledger service
rows.body_text: After two weeks of comparison we are picking Postgres 16 for the ledger service. Rationale: jsonb support, mature replication tooling, and the team already operates Postgres for billing. Decision is final; migration target is end of Q2.

rows.subject: Re: Postgres decision — confirmed at architecture review
rows.body_text: Architecture review signed off on the Postgres-for-ledger decision. No dissent. We'll start the migration plan next week.

rows.subject: On-call rotation policy update
rows.body_text: Effective March 15: weekday on-call shifts shorten from 7 days to 5 days. Weekend coverage stays the same. Pager rotation tool will reflect this automatically; no action needed unless you swap with someone.

rows.subject: This week's lunch menu
rows.body_text: Monday: lasagna. Tuesday: salad bar. Wednesday: tacos. Thursday: ramen. Friday: pizza. Allergen info on the cafeteria portal.

rows.subject: Re: Code review process — final decision
rows.body_text: Locking in the new review policy: every PR requires two reviewers, one of whom must be on the owning team. Exception: trivial doc-only changes (one reviewer ok). Effective April 1.

rows.text: Quick reminder: Postgres for ledger is the decision; please stop re-litigating mysql in PR reviews. Read RFC-0042 if you missed the rationale.
Two-reviewer rule is now enforced by the bot. Trivial doc PRs still allow single-reviewer override; flag with the `docs-only` label.
Coffee machine on floor 3 is broken again. Submitting a ticket. Floor 5 still works.
Alice Chen will be on parental leave from June 1 through August 15. Her on-call shifts during that period will be redistributed to the rest of the rotation.

rows.subject: Reminder: weekly platform sync at 10am
rows.body_text: Your weekly meeting Platform Sync starts in 1 hour. Conference room Boreal.

rows.subject: Ledger migration kickoff
rows.body_text: Kicking off the Postgres-for-ledger migration. Initial cutover plan: shadow writes for two weeks, then cut reads, then cut writes. I'm leading; Bob is co-lead. Spec doc forthcoming.

rows.text: Falcon project retro: the migration succeeded but the rollout window was too tight. Next time give ourselves a 2-week buffer.
Closing out Falcon — final metrics: 99.7% successful cutover, 0.3% required manual intervention. Documenting lessons in the wiki.
Falcon code is now archived. Read-only repo at github.com/acme/falcon-archived.
I think we should reconsider mysql for ledger. Postgres has all this jsonb complexity we don't need.

workstreams.name: dnd
workstreams.description: Tuesday-night D&D campaign. Track party members, key NPCs, ongoing plot threads, items acquired, decisions about the campaign world.

rows.text: Decision from tonight's session: the party rejected the king's offer of fealty and will pursue the artifact independently. Aligning campaign for next session around that branch.
Alice the Bard found a Cloak of Many Tongues in the witch's hut. DM confirms it works on any humanoid language. I'm keeping it for charisma checks.
House rule update: critical hits now do max damage on the rolled die plus a normal damage roll. Replaces the double-dice rule. Effective immediately.
Quick reminder: my character (Thorne the Cleric) is dedicated to Pelor. Healing word, light, sacred flame all require sunlight reference in the description. I'll keep flagging if I forget.
We are skipping next week's session — half the party is traveling. Resuming March 18.
Found a lead on the artifact: the merchant Calidor at the night market knows its last owner. The party agreed to meet him next session.
Calidor the merchant turned out to be working for the same cult the party fought in chapter 2. He escaped. Active plot thread: track Calidor.
Alice Chen (different from Alice the Bard) joined the campaign tonight as a backup player. Will play Lyra, a half-elf rogue, when someone needs to skip a session.
Ruling tonight on Cloak of Many Tongues: it only translates spoken language, not written. Alice the Bard kept asking. Locking this in so we stop relitigating.
Crit rule reminder: max die + normal roll. NOT double dice. We had three crits tonight and people kept doing it the old way.

rows.subject: Next session prep — what to bring
rows.body_text: Bring your latest character sheet. We'll start with the cult tracking arc and the visit to the night market. Snacks: I have chips, please bring something else if you want variety.

rows.text: What's everyone watching this week? I just finished season 2 of the new sci-fi show.
